exact_location.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

assessment.text: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.
assessment.rationale: Rewritten to narrower supported wording.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

assessment.text: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.
assessment.rationale: Rewritten to narrower supported wording.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

assessment.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped post-traumatic red eye, reduced acuity, fixed pupil, photophobia, and severe-pain warning fields.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Section 6 — red flags

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

assessment.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped trauma, chemical, foreign-body, visual, conjunctival, corneal, and pupil documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped return and referral documentation already covered by primary sources.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8–9 — management and referral criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8–9 — management and referral criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

additions.rationale: Supports mapped return and referral documentation already covered by primary sources.

rewrites.final_wording: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

source.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table
source.population: People with eye presentations requiring emergency, urgent, or priority referral triage.
source.exclusions: Exact for listed eye red flags and urgency categories; local UAE referral pathways and timing require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Annex 4 — anterior eye and traumatic red-eye referrals

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

rewrites.final_wording: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

rewrites.final_wording: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Corneal abrasion
source.population: People with suspected corneal abrasion, including trauma- and contact-lens-associated presentations.
source.exclusions: Exact for corneal-abrasion history, symptoms, fluorescein assessment, and referral context; UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Corneal abrasion
source.population: People with suspected corneal abrasion, including trauma- and contact-lens-associated presentations.
source.exclusions: Exact for corneal-abrasion history, symptoms, fluorescein assessment, and referral context; UAE pathways require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Clinical information and patient summary — causes and symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

rewrites.final_wording: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis
source.population: Adults and children presenting with red eye or discharge; serious or atypical eye presentations require face-to-face assessment or referral.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth conjunctivitis and red-eye documentation; partial for broad eye pain, visual change, contact-lens complications, and trauma.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5.1–5.9 — infectious, allergic, and non-infectious features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

rewrites.previous_wording: no foreign body sensation reported if applicable

rewrites.final_wording: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
